forbidden_actions.description: bin/naia-agent skeleton shipped end-to-end via Slices 3-XR-E through 3-XR-O (CHANGELOG line 1 ledger). Original gate '#3 (G01 bin/naia-agent) close' satisfied multiple times over. Kept as historical record; no longer enforced.
migration/phase-d branch (Phase B + C.2 = 189 unit test) push/merge forbidden. PAUSED state preserved.
projects/refs/ref-* — all files read-only. submodule clean state required.
projects/refs/ref-cc-cleanroom (ghuntley) code copying/redistribution forbidden. Pattern reference only.
projects/refs/ref-cc (nextain private) — original source re-extraction attempts require user approval.
docs/agent-loop-design.md D1~D8 decisions immutable. New decisions only via ref-adoption-matrix.md §D.
alpha-adk/.agents/progress/naia-4repo-migration-plan.md Part A immutable. This repo R0 only changes execution sequencing.

forbidden_actions.status: MOOT (RESOLVED — all R0/P0 sub-issues #3/#4/#5/#6 long since closed)
forbidden_actions.description: Originally: if any OPEN P0 sub-issue existed, R1 plan authoring blocked. All R0 P0 sub-issues closed; bin shipped through Slice 3-XR-O.

ref-cc-cleanroom (ghuntley) sole-dependency forbidden. When borrowing D01 (DANGEROUS_COMMANDS regex) / D02 (Path normalize) style patterns, cross-reference ≥1 OWASP/RFC/Anthropic 1st-party spec doc. Direct line-level copy from cleanroom forbidden (B22). Rationale: cc-cleanroom-deep-audit F1~F12 LLM hallucination/silent drift + repo archived 2025-03 + 2 commits only.
forbidden_actions.enforcement: PR description must include 'OWASP/RFC source cited' checkbox (added at Slice 2 entry via PR template)

forbidden_actions.description: @anthropic-ai/sdk minor-or-higher version bump PRs require fixture-replay re-record + StreamPlayer playback verification. Detects SDK breaking changes pre-merge.

required_actions_for_slice_pr.description: Each slice PR must add ≥1 new executable command (e.g. pnpm exec naia-agent ...).
Integration verification ≥1 (fixture-replay or real-LLM smoke or live backend call). Absence = merge blocked.
In CI without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, all tests must pass via fixture-replay alone. Real-LLM smoke is opt-in (KEY present). Real API keys never committed to fixtures.

matrix_id_citation.rule: Commit message + PR description must cite matrix IDs (A##/B##/C##/D##/E##/F##/G##)

workfile_lifecycle._inheritance_mode: inherit — values mirrored from root SoT. On root SoT update, refresh this block.
workfile_lifecycle.principle: All work files (progress reports, scratch scripts, temp data) MUST follow explicit lifecycle. No accumulation without policy.

directories..agents/context/: Permanent context (on_demand loaded). Long-lived. In-place corrections only. Never delete.
directories..agents/progress/: Active work reports + completed reports. JSON files = harness 1st-class (session-inject reads). MD reports = lifecycle-tracked.
directories..agents/progress/archive/YYYY-MM/: Completed reports moved here (preserved + searchable). readdirSync skips subdirs = automatically excluded from session-inject/commit-guard active set.
directories..agents/work/: Temporary work scripts. gitignored at root (.gitignore: .agents/work/). User-managed cleanup (no AI auto-delete).

workfile_lifecycle.completion_criteria: A work file is 'complete' when 2+ of: (a) related code committed/pushed to origin, (b) user explicitly declares done/closed, (c) cross-review GO + user acceptance, (d) superseded by next-step plan/issue. AI self-declaring 'complete' is prohibited.

triggers.work_start: Create or update one progress file (1 per work, not 1 per session).
triggers.after_commit_push: Move that work's progress to .agents/progress/archive/YYYY-MM/ (selective — mention in commit message).
triggers.monthly_cleanup: Review 30+ day progress files; archive completed or inline-resolve.

workfile_lifecycle.naming: Existing convention: <topic>-YYYY-MM-DD.md. On archive: move only (no rename) to preserve cross-references.
workfile_lifecycle.archive_unit: Archive MD/JSON pair together (same base name). If JSON has current_phase != close, set to close OR unbind before move — otherwise session-inject still treats it active (codex correction).
workfile_lifecycle.inbound_references: Default: allow link breakage on archive (archived = historical). On case-by-case need: leave 1-line stub at original path pointing to archive/YYYY-MM/<file> (user decision). No automatic archive index — over-engineering.
workfile_lifecycle.harness_scope_asymmetry: session-inject collects cwd + IMMEDIATE child subdirs only (projects/<sub>/.agents/progress/). 2nd-level subs not scanned. commit-guard scans cwd only. Policy applies repo-wide as convention but ENFORCEMENT range is root + direct children. 2nd-level subs need own hooks for enforcement (manual convention until then).
workfile_lifecycle.session_map_cleanup: On archive: .agents/progress/.session-map.json entries pointing to archived files become stale. session-inject auto-prunes on next run, but explicit cleanup is cleaner (jq or manual edit).

OPENCODE.md.note: opencode reads AGENTS.md directly; mirror exists for explicitness

CODEX.md.note: Codex reads AGENTS.md directly per OpenAI convention; mirror exists for explicitness

naia_self_tool.rationale: We design our own tool to follow AAIF standard; no NAIA.md needed

modes.--check: exit 1 if any mirror diverges (CI / pre-commit)

sync_mechanism.edit_rule: Edit ONLY AGENTS.md. Direct edits to mirrors are overwritten on next sync.
sync_mechanism.banner: Each mirror starts with HTML comment <!-- AUTO-GENERATED MIRROR --> (hidden in Markdown render, visible to LLMs/grep)

tool_specific_dirs.rule: Tool-specific behavior (hooks, slash commands, settings) lives in .{tool}/ directory, not in <TOOL>.md mirror
tool_specific_dirs.rationale: Mirror contains only the canonical project rules (same as AGENTS.md). Tool-specific extras are isolated in tool-specific dirs.

conflict_resolution: If AGENTS.md and mirror disagree → run sync script (AGENTS.md wins)
If .agents/context/agents-rules.json and AGENTS.md disagree → JSON wins (machine-readable, more precise)
If tool-specific dir setting and AGENTS.md disagree → tool-specific dir wins WITHIN tool's scope only (does not override forbidden_actions)
